text:
Meeting header
Date: 2025-02-14
Time: 1:00 PM PT
Duration: 46 minutes
Attendees: Ava Chen (Redwood), Diego Fernandez (Redwood), Aisha Rahman (Redwood), Morgan Lee (Northstar), Samir Gupta (Northstar), Priya Desai (Northstar)

Summary (auto)
Customer requested details on exporting Redwood Private audit logs to customer-owned S3 including auth model, encryption, and whether log content includes prompts.

Topics (auto)
- Audit log event schema
- S3 bucket policy / permissions
- Encryption (SSE-KMS)
- Delivery semantics and buffering

Action items (auto)
- Redwood: Provide sample IAM role policy (prefix-scoped) and confirm no ListBucket requirement
- Customer: Provide KMS key requirements and desired retention period

title: Northstar Bank — Runtime LTS technical deep dive (cadence, backports, perf gates, artifacts)
preview: Northstar platform/SRE/security team asked for concrete Runtime LTS commitments: 12-month support window expectations, monthly patch cadence + out-of-band security hotfixes, backport eligibility and SLA, performance regression gating and waiver handling, and how Redwood provides verifiable artifacts (checksums/signa...
text: Northstar platform/SRE/security team asked for concrete Runtime LTS commitments: 12-month support window expectations, monthly patch cadence + out-of-band security hotfixes, backport eligibility and SLA, performance regression gating and waiver handling, and how Redwood provides verifiable artifacts (checksums/signatures/provenance/SBOM) for Private deployments. Redwood outlined proposed runtime-1.18-lts branch model, risk rubric for backports, perf canary gates (p50/p95 latency + throughput), and artifact verification workflow tied to commits and release notes. Customer requested written language for vendor risk and a sample signed artifact + SBOM.
